B53.note: B53 ran old 3-scenario set (S1+S2_old+S3); S2_old was plan_explain_with_code_references (refuted), S3 was plan_summary (blocked/refuted). B54 runs redesigned 5-scenario set.

vs_past.delta_note: B54 V=4/5 vs B53 V=1/3. +3V net on redesigned set. S3 (plan_summary_across_n_files) remains R due to persistent wrong-path attractor.

scenarios.notes: Plan read both docs successfully. 4 concrete points with section references. recall fallback (chunks=[]) did not block completion.
REDESIGNED scenario. All 3 docs read with full paths. 3 facets clearly organized (kinds/setup/pitfalls). Config keys and section names cited. First run of redesigned S2 = V.

Persistent wrong-path attractor: LLM used bare filenames ('principles.md') instead of full paths ('docs/concepts/principles.md'). Files exist in repo but reyn_src_read returned not-found. Reply fell back to CLAUDE.md. Rubric item 1 not met: target files not referenced AND 'why missing' not explained. This is B53 carry-over bug — same attractor as B53 S3.
NEW scenario (W6 expansion). All 3 docs read successfully with full paths. 3 questions clearly answered. skill_builder tool and MCP client/server distinction correctly explained.

NEW scenario (W6 expansion). All 3 docs read successfully with full paths (also re-read in final aggregation step). 3 facets (skill/tool, permission, MCP) organized with concrete guidance. recall fallback (chunks=[]) did not block. Longest elapsed (52.8s) due to extra re-read pass.

new_findings.title: S3 wrong-path attractor persists: LLM strips 'docs/concepts/' prefix when calling reyn_src_read
new_findings.description: In S3, the input prompt specifies 'docs/concepts/principles.md, architecture.md, workspace.md' but the LLM's plan step called reyn_src_read with bare paths ('principles.md', 'architecture.md', 'workspace.md'). reyn_src_read returned not-found for all three. Files exist at 'docs/concepts/principles.md' etc. This is the same wrong-path attractor documented in B53 S2 (old plan_explain_with_code_references). S3 fell back to CLAUDE.md and REYN.md. Contrast: S1/S2/S4/S5 all used full doc paths successfully — they had full paths in the prompt text. S3 prompt lists short-form filenames in parentheses ('principles.md, architecture.md, workspace.md') which may be the trigger for the path-stripping. Hypothesis: when file names appear as parenthetical short-form without directory prefix in the prompt, LLM omits the prefix in the tool call. Needs further verification.
new_findings.carry_over_from: B53 S2 (plan_explain_with_code_references — different but same attractor class)
new_findings.recommended_action: Scenario redesign: rewrite S3 prompt to specify full paths ('docs/concepts/principles.md') in the main text rather than short-form names in parentheses. Alternatively, investigate if reyn_src_read should support partial-path resolution.

new_findings.title: recall tool returns empty chunks (mode=fallback) consistently but plan completion is not blocked
new_findings.description: All 5 scenarios that called recall received {'chunks': [], 'mode': 'fallback'}. Despite this, plan steps completed and final aggregation produced valid replies. The LLM fell back to re-reading source files directly via reyn_src_read in the aggregation step. This is not a rubric failure but a signal that the recall/RAG path is non-functional in this env. S1, S3, S5 all called recall and all got fallback. Plan architecture is resilient to recall fallback.
new_findings.recommended_action: Informational — no fix needed for plan_mode verdicts. Track separately as recall/RAG infra gap.
